skill.name: firebase-development-add-feature
skill.description: Guides test-first development for adding Firebase features, including Cloud Functions, API endpoints, Firestore collections, security rules, and emulator verification.
skill.summary: Add Firebase features with tests, security rules, authentication checks, and emulator validation.
skill.author: 2389-research

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many high-risk patterns, but review found they are Markdown examples, inline commands, local references, and localhost emulator URLs. No malicious intent, prompt injection, credential access, or data exfiltration evidence was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Local Developer Command Guidance
low_findings.description: The external command findings are mostly Markdown code fences and inline examples for ls, grep, npm tests, Firebase emulators, and opening localhost. These commands support normal Firebase development workflows and do not show command injection or hidden execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged commands are visible documentation examples in SKILL.md. They are local project inspection, build, test, and emulator commands with no dynamic input or remote payload.

low_findings.title: Localhost Emulator URL
low_findings.description: The network finding points to http://127.0.0.1:4000, the Firebase Emulator UI. This is a local development address, not an external data destination.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is a loopback address used after firebase emulators:start. No evidence shows outbound exfiltration or contact with a third-party service.

low_findings.title: Example Relative Import Path
low_findings.description: The path traversal findings come from a TypeScript test import using ../../tools/yourFeature. It is an example relative module import, not filesystem traversal against user-controlled paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The sequence appears inside a static test snippet. There is no runtime file read, path concatenation, or user input controlling the path.

low_findings.title: Static Pattern False Positives in Markdown
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and Ruby backtick findings are not confirmed by semantic review. They appear to be triggered by Markdown syntax, ordinary terms, shell examples, and documentation references.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of the cited lines found instructional text and code examples, not cryptographic operations or malicious reconnaissance. No prompt injection text was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Commands Mentioned in Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs agents to run local inspection, test, build, and emulator commands. This is expected for a Firebase coding workflow, but users should review commands before execution in sensitive repositories.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command guidance is explicit and local. It is not hidden execution, but it remains a relevant operational risk factor for marketplace users.

content.value_statement: Adding Firebase features can break tests, security rules, or authentication flows. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through test-first implementation and emulator verification.
actual_capabilities: Classifies Firebase feature types before implementation begins.
Guides test-first development for handlers, endpoints, and collections.
Adds authentication checks and Firestore security rule steps.
Defines consistent success, message, and data response patterns.
Requires build, test, and emulator verification before completion.
Points to project examples for architecture, auth, rules, and emulators.
limitations: It assumes an existing Firebase project is already configured.
It does not perform initial project setup or dependency installation.
It provides workflow guidance, not a runnable Firebase framework.
It depends on the project having tests and emulator scripts available.

use_cases.title: Add an authenticated API endpoint
use_cases.description: Implement a new Express route with tests, auth checks, response formatting, and emulator validation.

use_cases.title: Create a protected Firestore collection
use_cases.description: Design collection rules, indexes, validation tests, and server-write patterns for a new data model.

use_cases.title: Extend Cloud Functions safely
use_cases.description: Add callable, scheduled, or trigger functions while following existing architecture and verification steps.

prompt_templates.title: Add a simple Firebase feature
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a guided implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to add a Firebase feature for [feature]. Start by identifying the feature type and writing the first failing test.

prompt_templates.title: Create a secure collection
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for Firestore rules and tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a new Firestore collection named [collection]. Include security rules, index guidance, tests, and emulator verification steps.

prompt_templates.title: Implement an authenticated endpoint
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for API behavior and auth enforcement
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement an authenticated Firebase endpoint for [operation]. Follow existing architecture, add tests first, enforce auth, and verify with emulators.

prompt_templates.title: Ship a complete feature workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request across code, tests, rules, and verification
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to build [feature] end to end. Preserve project patterns, update exports, add rules and indexes, run tests, and report verification results.

output_examples.input: Add a new endpoint that creates a project for the current user.
output: A feature classification and implementation checklist.
A failing test plan before production code changes.
Auth, response pattern, export, and emulator verification steps.

output_examples.input: Create a Firestore collection for team invitations.
output: Security rule guidance for authenticated reads and restricted writes.
Index guidance for common invitation queries.
Integration test expectations for emulator validation.

output_examples.input: Add a scheduled function that cleans expired records.
output: A scheduled function workflow matched to existing project architecture.
Build, test, and emulator checks before marking the work complete.

best_practices: Write the failing test before implementation work starts.
Match the existing Firebase architecture and authentication model.
Verify behavior with Firebase emulators before declaring completion.
anti_patterns: Adding endpoints before defining authentication and validation behavior.
Changing tests to match broken implementation instead of fixing code.
Skipping Firestore rules or emulator checks for new collections.

faq.question: Does this skill create a new Firebase project?
faq.answer: No. It is for adding features to an existing Firebase project.

faq.question: Which Firebase features does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers Cloud Functions, HTTP endpoints, callable functions, scheduled functions, triggers, and Firestore collections.

faq.question: Does it require test-driven development?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow asks for failing tests before implementation.

faq.question: Does it include Firestore security rules?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes rule guidance for server-write and client-write collection patterns.

faq.question: Can it run Firebase commands automatically?
faq.answer: It provides command guidance. Tool execution depends on user approval and the active AI environment.

faq.question: Is this safe for production repositories?
faq.answer: It is workflow guidance with local commands. Review generated changes and commands before applying them to production code.
